sourceNote: AFM-only training transcription from Viking Air PSM 1-63-1A Revision 53, Section 3.10.4, page 3-34. This subsection contains no bold memory actions. An engine flameout is first handled using Engine Failure During Flight; a normal airstart is considered only after a correctable cause is corrected. Crew-role fields are trainer UI assignments only; they are not approved PF/PM callouts or operator SOP. Verify applicability against the aircraft-specific AFM, amendments, supplements, and operator procedures.

flow.action: Complete the Engine Failure During Flight checklist, AFM 3.3.4.
If the cause of the flameout can be corrected, correct it. Examples include improper fuel management or failure to extend the intake deflectors in visible moisture at +5°C or less.
After correcting the cause, a normal airstart may be attempted using AFM 3.3.5.
